findings.title: Potential ReDoS vulnerability
findings.description:
Regex with nested quantifiers may cause catastrophic backtracking, leading to denial of service.

**Analysis:**
🔍 Pattern issue: nested quantifiers detected
📦 In function: `get_go_mod_version` (1 callers)
findings.why_it_matters:
ReDoS attacks exploit regex backtracking to consume CPU:
• A malicious input can take exponential time to evaluate
• Single requests can freeze the server
• Input like 'aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa!' on /(a+)+$/ can hang

findings.title: Single point of failure: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/starship/src/modules/mod.rs::handle:118` dominates 332 functions (16%)
findings.description: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/starship/src/modules/mod.rs::handle:118` dominates 332 of 2130 functions (15.6%). PageRank percentile: 0%. Blast radius boundary: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/starship/src/config.rs::impl<StarshipConfig>::get_config:191, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/starship/src/config.rs::impl<StarshipConfig>::get_module_config:178, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/starship/src/config.rs::parse_style_string:365, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/starship/src/context.rs::home_dir:498, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/starship/src/context.rs::impl<Context<'a>>::dir_contents:389 (+57 more)
findings.suggested_fix: Consider splitting this function into smaller, independently callable units. Extract sub-functionality behind interfaces so callers have alternative paths.
findings.why_it_matters: A single point of failure means all dependent code paths funnel through one function. If this function has a bug, performance issue, or API change, the blast radius is disproportionately large.

findings.title: unsafe block without SAFETY comment
findings.description: Unsafe blocks should document why they're safe. Add a `// SAFETY:` comment.
findings.why_it_matters: Unsafe code bypasses Rust's safety guarantees. SAFETY comments are essential for code review.

findings.title: Panic risk: unwrap() without context
findings.description: Using `unwrap()` can cause panics. Consider `?`, `unwrap_or`, or proper error handling.
findings.why_it_matters: Panics crash the program without recovery. Using proper error handling makes code more robust and debuggable.

findings.title: Circular dependency: 3 files
findings.description:
Mutual dependency between: config.rs ↔ context.rs ↔ utils.rs

**Weakest link to break:** `config.rs` → `context.rs` (1 import)
findings.suggested_fix:
🔗 **Best place to break:** `config.rs` → `context.rs` (weakest coupling: 1 imports)

Since `config.rs` only imports 1 symbol(s) from `context.rs`, consider:
- Move those symbols to a shared module
- Pass them as parameters instead of importing
- Use dependency injection

**For small cycles:**
1. Merge tightly coupled modules if they're always used together
2. Use TYPE_CHECKING (Python) or type-only imports (TS) for type hints
3. Extract common code to a third module
findings.why_it_matters: Circular dependencies make code harder to understand, test, and maintain. They can cause import errors at runtime and make it difficult to refactor individual modules.

findings.title: High panic density in `print_configuration`: 4 panic-family calls
findings.description: [COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Function `print_configuration` contains 4 calls to `.unwrap()`, `.expect()`, or `panic!()` at line 82, line 89, line 111, line 129. This makes the function fragile and likely to panic at runtime.
findings.suggested_fix:
Reduce panic points by:
- Using `?` operator for error propagation
- Using `unwrap_or` / `unwrap_or_default` / `unwrap_or_else`
- Returning `Result<T, E>` instead of panicking
- Extracting fallible logic into a helper that returns Result
findings.why_it_matters: Functions with many panic points are fragile. A single unexpected None/Err will crash the program. Consolidating error handling makes code more robust and easier to reason about.

findings.title: Mutual recursion: 2 functions in call cycle (complexity 29)
findings.description: Mutual recursion detected: 2 functions form a call cycle. Cycle: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/starship/src/formatter/string_formatter.rs::impl<StringFormatter<'a>>::new:67 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/starship/src/formatter/string_formatter.rs::impl<StringFormatter<'a>>::parse:236. Aggregate complexity: 29.
findings.suggested_fix: Consider refactoring to eliminate direct mutual calls. Common strategies: merge the two functions, use a callback parameter, or introduce a shared data structure that both functions operate on.
findings.why_it_matters: Mutual recursion creates tight coupling between functions, making them impossible to understand, test, or refactor independently. It can also cause stack overflows if the recursion depth is unbounded.

findings.title: Structural bridge: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/starship/src/modules/directory.rs::contract_path:215` separates components of [2077, 23]
findings.description: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/starship/src/modules/directory.rs::contract_path:215` is a structural bridge. Removing it would split the graph into components of sizes [2077, 23]. All communication between these components currently flows through this single node.
findings.suggested_fix: Reduce coupling through this node by introducing alternative call paths, extracting shared interfaces, or splitting the bridge function into independently accessible units.
findings.why_it_matters: A structural bridge is a single node whose failure disconnects entire subsystems. This creates fragile architecture where a bug, API change, or refactor in one function can cascade to all dependent components.

findings.title: Regex compiled inside loop
findings.description:
Regex compiled in loop (loop starts line 319).

**Performance impact:** Regex compilation is expensive. In a loop of N iterations, this costs N compilations instead of 1.
findings.suggested_fix:
Move regex compilation outside the loop:
```rust
let re = Regex::new(pattern)?;  // Before loop
for item in items {
re.is_match(item);  // Reuse
}
```
findings.why_it_matters: Regex compilation involves parsing and optimization. Doing it once instead of N times can dramatically improve performance.

findings.title: Long method: parse (170 lines)
findings.description:
Function 'parse' has 170 lines (effective threshold: 150).

**Graph Analysis:**
Orchestrator pattern: calls 13 functions (reduced severity)
Calls 3 different modules - possible split points
findings.suggested_fix:
This appears to be an orchestrator function (coordinates many calls).
If it must remain long, ensure it:
1. Has clear section comments
2. Handles errors at each step
3. Has a clear flow (consider a state machine for complex flows)
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.

findings.title: Excessive nesting: 8 levels in parse
findings.description:
8 levels of nesting (threshold: 7).

**Analysis:**
In function: `parse`
High complexity: 27 (nesting compounds this)
Contains 2 match/switch level(s) (discounted from raw depth 10)
Existing helpers that could reduce nesting: clone_without_meta

Threshold: 5 (adaptive) | Actual: 8 | Default would be: 5 | Profile: p90=2, p95=3, mean=1.0, n=2109
findings.suggested_fix:
This function already has helpers like `clone_without_meta`. Consider:
1. Extract more nested blocks into similar helpers
2. Use guard clauses (early returns) to reduce nesting
3. Replace nested ifs with switch/match
findings.why_it_matters: Deep nesting makes code hard to read and maintain. Each level increases cognitive load exponentially.

findings.title: Long method: module (154 lines)
findings.description:
Function 'module' has 154 lines (effective threshold: 150).

**Graph Analysis:**
Calls 3 different modules - possible split points
findings.suggested_fix:
This function calls 3 different modules. Consider extracting:
  - `handle_formatter()` for formatter operations
  - `handle_src()` for src operations
  - `handle_modules()` for modules operations
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.
